perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Quality Control, Licensor Approvals. Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right at all times to control and approve the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon), and to inspect Licensee's business operations upon reasonable prior notice for the purpose of ensuring that a high level of quality of the Licensed Products is being maintained by Licensee.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Quality Control, Licensor Consultation. Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right at all times to consult and advise on the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon), and may inquire about Licensee's business operations for informational purposes, without any right to control or approve the operational procedures of the Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Licensor Approvals' to 'Licensor Consultation' and replacing 'control and approve' with 'consult and advise' significantly weakens Licensor's rights regarding quality control. This creates a contradiction with the later statement in the same section that 'The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor.' as the consultation and advise makes the Licensor not being able to enforce the quality in the same extent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor. If the quality of a class of the Licensed Products falls below such standards, Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to restore such quality. In the event that Licensee has not taken appropriate steps to restore such quality within one-hundred twenty (120) days after notification by Licensor, Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Licensor the right to 'control and approve' the quality of Licensed Products, ensuring they met specific standards. The changed text weakens this to 'consult and advise,' removing the Licensor's power to enforce quality standards. The contradicted text states that 'The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor.' This creates a contradiction because the Licensor no longer has the authority to 'approve' samples effectively, making the quality standard stated in the contradicted text unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee agrees to reasonably cooperate with Licensor in achieving registration of the Licensed Mark worldwide, and in maintaining and protecting existing registrations therefor at Licensor's sole expense. Licensee shall execute any and all documents which Licensor may reasonably request in support of such registrations, and, at Licensor's request, Licensee shall provide use evidence, testimony, and documentation that may be required in any ex parte or inter partes administrative proceedings and prosecutions, maintenance and renewals involving registrations of the Licensed Mark, at Licensee's sole expense.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may, at its discretion, cooperate with Licensor in achieving registration of the Licensed Mark worldwide, and in maintaining and protecting existing registrations therefor at Licensor's sole expense. Licensee will endeavor to execute documents if reasonably convenient and if it doesn't disrupt normal operations, and may provide use evidence, testimony, and documentation as available and at Licensee’s convenience.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to reasonably cooperate' to 'may, at its discretion, cooperate' and weakening the obligation to execute documents and provide evidence introduces ambiguity. While Licensor bears the expense, the level of Licensee's cooperation is now discretionary. This creates a contradiction with Section 2, Enforcement of Intellectual Property, where Licensee is required to promptly notify Licensor of any infringement. If Licensee chooses not to cooperate in registration, it diminishes Licensor's ability to enforce those rights, therefore contradicting the obligation to notify and potentially creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If legally able and without breaching any confidentiality provisions of a contract with a third party, in the event that Licensee becomes aware that any third party is infringing the Licensed Mark, Licensee shall promptly notify Licensor and provide pertinent details. Licensor shall have the right in its sole discretion to bring a legal action for infringement against the third party, together with the right to enforce and collect any judgment thereon. If Licensor elects to exercise such right, Licensee shall, at Licensor's request, provide reasonable assistance to Licensor, at the sole expense of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the Licensee's obligation to cooperate in achieving and maintaining the Licensed Mark's registration, shifting from 'agrees to reasonably cooperate' to 'may, at its discretion, cooperate.' This directly contradicts the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' section where Licensee 'shall promptly notify Licensor' of any infringement and 'provide pertinent details.' If the Licensee is not obligated to cooperate fully in registration and maintenance (as per the changed text), the Licensor's ability to enforce the mark is diminished. This creates a direct conflict because diminished ability to enforce infringes on the Licensee's obligation to notify the Licensor of infringement, creating a contradiction between obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Licensor Indemnitees agree to cooperate with Licensee, at Licensee's expense, to provide copies of any documents or materials reasonably requested by Licensee in support of its defense of the Licensor Indemnitees.
perturbation.changed_text: The Licensor Indemnitees will attempt to cooperate with Licensee, resources permitting, to provide copies of documents if readily available to aid in Licensee's defense.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agree to cooperate' to 'will attempt to cooperate, resources permitting' introduces uncertainty regarding the Licensor Indemnitees' obligation to provide support to the Licensee in defending claims. This weakens the Licensor Indemnitees' commitment to cooperate, making their support conditional. This change introduces an In-Text contradiction in that the Licensee is being put in a position to defend the Licensor Indemnitees and those same indemnitees aren't giving the Licensee all the tools nessesary to defend them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensor and its officers, directors, employees, agents, corporate subsidiaries, parents, and affiliates ("Licensor Indemnitees") from and against any and all demands, claims, actions or causes of action, assessments, deficiencies, damages, losses, liabilities and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable expenses of investigation and attorneys' fees and expenses), incurred in conjunction with or arising out of or relating to any third-party claim concerning the Licensed Products and any acts or omissions of Licensee with respect to the Licensed Mark, including without limitation Licensee's performance of its obligations under this Agreement. The Licensor Indemnitees will attempt to cooperate with Licensee, resources permitting, to provide copies of documents if readily available to aid in Licensee's defense.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Licensor Indemnitees 'agree to cooperate,' implying a firm commitment. The changed text weakens this commitment to 'will attempt to cooperate, resources permitting,' making cooperation conditional. This directly contradicts the 'Licensee shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensor' clause because the Licensor Indemnitees are not fully committed to providing the necessary support (documents) for their own defense, which the Licensee is responsible for. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the Licensee's obligation to defend is undermined by the Licensor Indemnitees' conditional cooperation. The original text ensured that the indemnitees would have the tools to defend them, while the new text makes the availability of those tools conditional, and therefore contradicts the original text.
